name: Arroz de Pato
description: Portuguese duck rice — slow-braised duck cooked with wine and spices, then baked with rice, chouriço, and bacon.

recipeInstructions.text: Place duck pieces in a large pot with onion, carrots, bay leaf, and wine. Cover with water, season, and simmer for 90 minutes until the duck is very tender.
Remove duck pieces and shred the meat from the bones. Strain the broth and reserve.
Measure the broth and cook the rice in it (2 parts broth to 1 part rice) until just under-done.
Preheat oven to 200°C. In an oven dish, layer half the rice, all the shredded duck, then the remaining rice. Top with chouriço and bacon slices.
Bake uncovered for 20–25 minutes until the top is golden and slightly crispy. Serve hot.

notes.title: Make-ahead
notes.text: The duck can be braised a day ahead — the flavours deepen overnight.
